mechanism: Each landing zone gets a dedicated VNet with a CIDR from IPAM. PaaS services connect via service endpoints — private endpoints are not used because they add fixed hourly cost.
why: Shared VNets couple network lifecycles — address space changes or NSG modifications in one landing zone affect another, requiring cross-team coordination.

related.note: VNet is a self-contained capability per landing zone.
VNet lifecycle-managed within the landing-zone-resources stack.
Public access denied — VNet is the only network path.
Private endpoints carry fixed hourly cost — service endpoints are the alternative.
IPAM allocates the address space — no manual CIDR.

violations: Service using a private endpoint — fixed hourly cost.
VNet provisioned outside the landing-zone-resources stack.
